text.title: mcp-gateway-composition Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mcp-gateway-composition as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mixing virtual-server curation with per-client visibility in one config block (filter rules apply to a tool that has been renamed by curation).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deny rules become ambiguous — the renamed tool may match neither the original nor the new name, silently leaking access.

failure-mode.description: Federation gateway that re-exposes downstream tools without re-namespacing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two team gateways both named search collide at the org level. Agent picks at random.

failure-mode.description: Adding a gateway in front of 2 servers "for future-proofing".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Below the threshold the gateway is pure latency and one more thing that can break. Add it when the threshold is crossed, not preemptively.

failure-mode.description: Treating the gateway as a security boundary while letting agents bypass it via direct MCP connections.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If clients can dial servers directly, the gateway's deny rules are decorative. Enforce gateway-only access at the network layer.
